summary: Replace the generic student-detail close button with a named back-to-source action so staff can tell which real worklist or dashboard will reopen before leaving detail.
selectedReason: Current repo evidence showed that `siteStudentDetailState.sourceSection` already preserved the originating section and close already returned there, but `renderSiteStudentDetailSurface()` still exposed only a generic `Close` button. This was the smallest safe navigation upgrade because it reused the existing authorized detail drawer, required no new routes or data, and improved role-based usability across Students, Site Dashboard, Review Queue, Mentor Assignments, Mentor Dashboard, Program Dashboard, and Operations.

candidates.decision: deferred: backend queue rows are still absent
deferred: no exact current destination is proven for that follow-up row
deferred: mutation and audit design still needed
deferred: larger navigation batch than this detail-header slice
deferred: browser runtime proof is outside this local batch

candidates.candidate: Site selector follow-up for multi-site staff
candidates.decision: deferred: broader workflow than this navigation micro-slice
not selected: lower user pain than the cross-worklist detail dead end

nextRecommended: Revisit privacy-safe student-detail URL state, or clarify Review Queue missing-submission semantics only when scoped backend evidence exists.
doNotRepeat: Do not replace named student-detail return buttons with a generic Close control while sourceSection continues to preserve the originating worklist.
Do not add student-detail URL state until reload and authorization behavior are proven.
Do not broaden viewer, teacher, mentor, or student access while improving staff detail navigation.
